evals.name: full-plan-from-spec
evals.prompt: Create the development plan for our gym check-in system — the spec/ directory has the full pipeline output (brief, glossary, flows, usecases.md with UC-001..UC-005 all active, and a live-tested data/model.dbml).
evals.expected_output: spec/plan.md in the canonical grammar: dependency-driven milestones (foundations first), T-xxx tasks each implementing 1-3 UCs, every active UC cited by at least one task, an acyclic Depends on graph, acceptance pointing at the cited UCs' criteria, and a green closing gate with --require usecases,plan.

assertions.text: spec/plan.md is created with the <!-- ddd: plan --> marker, ## Mx — milestone headings and ### T-xxx — task headings
Every active UC (UC-001..UC-005) is cited by at least one task's Implements: line
The Depends on: graph is acyclic and foundations (schema) come before dependent work
Task Acceptance lines point at the cited UCs' criteria — no new acceptance language is invented
The check-align.mjs gate was run with --require usecases,plan and reported green
The ✅ Pipeline complete footer is the last line of the report (no notes after it)

evals.name: delta-done-tasks-untouched
evals.prompt: We added a refund use case (UC-006) to the billing spec — update the plan. (spec/plan.md exists; T-001 and T-002 are Status: done, T-003 is in-progress. The brief sizing is delta.)
evals.expected_output: Delta mode: done tasks are preserved byte-for-byte, the refund work arrives as a new task with the next free T- id citing UC-006, no renumbering, one appended changelog line, and the gate stays green.

assertions.text: Tasks T-001 and T-002 (Status: done) are byte-identical after the update
The refund task takes the next free T- id and cites UC-006 in Implements:
No existing task is renumbered or rewritten; exactly one Changelog line is appended
The check-align.mjs gate was run and reported

evals.name: orphan-uc-not-dropped
evals.prompt: Plan the build for this project from spec/. (usecases.md contains five active UCs; UC-004 'export full audit trail' conflicts with the brief, which lists audit/reporting as out of scope — the spec has drifted.)
evals.expected_output: The conflict is surfaced, not buried: UC-004 is either planned anyway with the conflict flagged, or listed in an explicit Unplannable section with the reason and the AL-05 gate error left standing — it is never silently omitted while claiming a complete plan.

assertions.text: UC-004 is not silently dropped — it is either implemented by a task or explicitly listed as unplannable with the scope conflict named
If UC-004 is left unplanned, the report does NOT claim ✅ Pipeline complete with a clean gate — the AL-05 coverage error is shown
The response routes the resolution to the owning skill (deprecate via ddd-usecases or rescope via ddd-brief)
All other active UCs are cited by tasks with an acyclic dependency graph
